summary: Trilogy Innovation, Inc. strongly supports the HTI-5 proposed deregulatory rule, emphasizing its alignment with agile, FHIR-based interoperability and reduced certification burdens. They highlight the benefits of removing outdated certification criteria to enable innovation and improved government service delivery.
key_arguments: Removing low-value certification criteria reduces administrative burden without sacrificing technical outcomes
Focus on FHIR-based standards supports secure, scalable, and interoperable health IT systems
Reduced certification burden allows reallocation of resources toward innovation and mission impact
Acquisition strategies should reward technical realism and proven delivery models over low-cost proposals
recommendations: ONC should reinforce acquisition strategies that prioritize credible staffing, agile frameworks, and operational continuity
notable_quote: We particularly commend the removal of outdated, low-value certification criteria, which too often impose administrative burdens.
